summary: The Pester unit tests provide reasonable coverage of core matrix generation logic (Cartesian product, excludes, includes, strategy options, validation) but critically fail to meet the explicit requirement that 'ALL TESTS MUST RUN THROUGH ACT'. Tests are run directly with Invoke-Pester, not via GitHub Actions workflow as mandated. Additionally, coverage of edge cases is sparse: no tests for type coercion with mixed string/numeric values, no tests of multiple includes merging into same combination, no tests of the CLI entry point with OutputPath parameter, no tests of the script's error handling in actual workflow context. The workflow file structure tests use brittle regex matching against YAML. Test design is clean (good Describe organization, proper fixture cleanup, test independence) but lacks integration with actual fixture JSON files referenced in Run-ActTests.ps1. This suite would catch major happy-path regressions in isolation but would not validate end-to-end GitHub Actions execution, subtle type-handling bugs, or complex include/exclude interaction edge cases that the `act` integration tests are meant to catch.
